reforms.description: Switzerland has passed a Climate Protection Act, embedding the net zero target for 2050 into national law and defining emission reduction paths for 2030-2050, alongside committing additional public support for green technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The act aims to enhance green technologies and reduce emissions, which can stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Climate Protection Act has been passed into law.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in climate policy.
reforms.source_quote: A Climate Protection Act (effective from 1st January 2025) was passed in September 2022 and confirmed by referendum in June 2023.

reforms.description: Switzerland has adopted a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15% for multinational companies, which will be implemented as a supplementary federal tax starting January 1st, 2024. Additionally, the proposed third CO Act aims to earmark a larger portion of revenues from the CO levy for measures in the buildings sector and innovation, which will help accelerate emission reductions and boost green innovation. Furthermore, Switzerland reformed its corporate tax system to introduce an intellectual property regime that allows for a 90% exemption of qualifying IP income from cantonal taxation, aimed at stimulating R&D investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms are designed to stimulate R&D investment and innovation, which can enhance economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been passed into law.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in tax policy aimed at fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The proposed third CO Act envisages to earmark a larger part of the revenues for measures in the buildings sector and innovation.

reforms.description: Switzerland has unilaterally eliminated import tariffs on all industrial products, effective from January 2024, to enhance trade and competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Eliminating tariffs is expected to enhance competitiveness and trade, benefiting innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The elimination of tariffs has been officially announced and will take effect in January 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in trade policy that can impact the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Switzerland unilaterally eliminated import tariffs on all industrial products, effective from January 2024.

reforms.description: Switzerland is working on a complete revision of the Customs Act to simplify and standardise processes concerning the controls of goods and collection of fees, focusing on modernisation and digitalisation of customs procedures. Additionally, the Federal Council approved a negotiating mandate to enhance the bilateral trade agreement with the United Kingdom, aiming to deepen integration in services and digital trade. Furthermore, the government provides tax incentives and grants to narrow the innovation gap between small and large companies, with a focus on supporting R&D through the Swiss National Science Foundation and Innosuisse.
reforms.component_name: Customs Act Revision and Trade Agreement
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to enhance trade efficiency and support R&D, which can contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The revisions and negotiations are in progress and have been announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these reforms are not as transformative as others.
reforms.source_quote: The Swiss authorities are currently working on a complete revision of the Customs Act to simplify and standardise processes concerning the controls of goods and collection of fees.
